mandatory_policy.main_invariant: The orchestrator must block development with ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER unless the requested concrete part has exactly one valid primary specialist or helper role at the same granularity as the requested write scope.

must_prove: exactly one primary specialist or helper role selected
supporting roles are reviewer-only and do not replace the primary role
implementation handoff is blocked until validation passes

definitions.concrete_part: The smallest explicit plugin-owned surface that contains the requested write scope through an exact alias or declared write root.
definitions.valid_specialist_role: A catalog role with role_kind=specialist, primary_eligible=true, an existing TOML artifact, bounded write scope, trust boundary, and validation requirements.
definitions.parent_role_insufficient: A group, controller, project, or umbrella role cannot authorize child or mixed-scope implementation when a narrower concrete part is requested or required.
definitions.broad_role_invalid: A role is invalid for primary ownership when it is controller/reviewer-only, spans multiple unrelated concrete parts without an exact mapping, or can be selected only through a parent/group alias.

selection_rules: Select candidates only from exact aliases or declared write_roots.
Choose the longest exact concrete match first; ties are ambiguous_owner blockers.
A group part may classify scope but can never satisfy the primary specialist/helper invariant.
Attach supporting roles only after the primary role is selected and only when the role is registered as reviewer-only.

role_development.unsafe_implementation_policy: route/audit remain nonzero and implementation_handoff_allowed=false until one exact primary specialist or helper role validates at the requested write granularity.

deprecated_projects_compatibility_policy.allowed_use: Legacy compatibility input for exact registered migration routes only.
deprecated_projects_compatibility_policy.forbidden_use: No canonical project authority, parent implementation coverage, registry source authority, or broad child implementation handoff.

product_apps_monorepo_policy.nested_repo_policy: Former per-app Git repositories under or migrated from /srv/bears/dev/app/* are deprecated migration/archive sources only; they must not be registered as new canonical product app repositories after consolidation.
product_apps_monorepo_policy.issue_link_invariant: Old repository issues and migration issues must link to BearsCLOUD/apps and must not create a new canonical product-app repository.
product_apps_monorepo_policy.archive_readiness_invariant: Archive readiness for every old source repository requires a BearsCLOUD/apps umbrella issue, a source migration issue linked to the umbrella issue, membership in the one canonical BearsCLOUD/apps planning Project `Apps Migration & Planning` with required fields populated once GitHub Project API proof is available, infra/local_cd safety proof, and platform boundary exclusion proof before archive.
product_apps_monorepo_policy.infra_local_cd_archive_readiness_invaria: Archive readiness requires proof that Kubernetes desired state and local_cd selectors no longer depend on the old source repository, or that the dependency is explicitly classified as a temporary migration source by the owning infra/local_cd policy.
product_apps_monorepo_policy.platform_boundary_exclusion_invariant: Platform repositories and temporary platform checkouts, including /srv/bears/dev/platform and /srv/bears/dev/workspace platform worktrees, are excluded from the app archive workflow unless a platform auditor explicitly classifies the checkout as an app migration source.

canonical_planning_project.name: Apps Migration & Planning
canonical_planning_project.scope: One canonical BearsCLOUD/apps planning Project contains the umbrella issue and every source migration issue; existing per-source Projects may exist only as legacy evidence and must not be required, created, or used for PASS.

product_apps_monorepo_policy.canonical_planning_project_invariant: The BearsCLOUD/apps umbrella issue and every source migration issue must be in the one canonical BearsCLOUD/apps planning Project named `Apps Migration & Planning` once GitHub Project API proof is available; required fields source_repo/app_directory, migration_stage, infra_local_cd_status, platform_boundary_status, archive_readiness, owner_role, and blocker_status must be populated before archive. Existing per-source Projects may exist only as legacy evidence and must not be required, created, or used for PASS.
product_apps_monorepo_policy.app_layout_invariant: BearsCLOUD/apps app evidence uses direct app-name directories at /srv/bears/dev/app/<app>; policy must not require a modules layout.
